title: Configure the Voice (TTS) for Your Agent
lede: Your Smart Agent's voice is its first impression — and iMash gives you hundreds to choose from. Pick a text-to-speech (TTS) provider, preview and filter voices by gender, accent, and age, fine-tune speed and emotion, set a backup voice, and even clone your own voice from a 10-second sample.

steps.title: Open Your Agent in the Editor
steps.content:
1. In the left sidebar, click Smart Agents (under Intelligence Center).
  2. Click the name of the agent you want to configure. The agent editor opens.
  3. If the editor opens in the node canvas, click Form in the view toggle to switch to the form view used in this guide.
💡 The middle column of the form view lists every capability panel of your agent — Knowledge Base, Transcriber (STT), Voice (TTS), Brains (LLM), and more. Each row shows its current selection as a green pill and an ON/OFF toggle.

images.alt: Smart Agent editor with the capability panels in the middle column
images.caption: The agent editor. The Voice (TTS) panel sits in the middle column.

images.alt: Smart Agent editor with the capability panels in the middle column
images.caption: The agent editor. The Voice (TTS) panel sits in the middle column.

steps.title: Open the Voice (TTS) Panel
steps.content:
1. In the middle column, find the Voice (TTS) row. The green pill next to it shows the current provider and voice (for example cartesia / Mexican Woman), followed by the backup voice if one is set.
  2. Make sure its toggle is set to ON — an agent with the voice turned off cannot speak on calls.
  3. Click the Voice (TTS) row. The voice picker opens as a large popup.

images.alt: Capability list zoomed on the Voice (TTS) row with its provider pill and ON toggle
images.caption: Click the Voice (TTS) row to open the voice picker. The pill shows the current provider and voice.

images.alt: Capability list zoomed on the Voice (TTS) row with its provider pill and ON toggle
images.caption: Click the Voice (TTS) row to open the voice picker. The pill shows the current provider and voice.

steps.title: Get to Know the Voice Picker
steps.content:
The Voice (TTS) popup is your voice studio. It has four zones:

  • Left rail — the list of TTS providers, each with a one-line tagline (e.g. "Ultra-low latency · Real-time"). The Clone Voice entry sits at the bottom of the rail.
  • Provider header (top of the right pane) — shows the active provider and hosts the Advanced Settings button (plus Cloned Voices when iMash TTS is active).
  • Filters and current voice — the Gender, Accent, and Age dropdowns on the left; the currently selected voice with its own play button on the right.
  • Voice list — a pageable table of every voice the active provider offers.

In the top-right corner, the Production | Backup switch lets you configure the main voice or the automatic fallback voice (more on that below).

images.alt: Voice (TTS) picker popup with provider rail, filters, current voice, and voice table
images.caption: The voice picker: providers on the left, filters and the voice list on the right.

images.alt: Voice (TTS) picker popup with provider rail, filters, current voice, and voice table
images.caption: The voice picker: providers on the left, filters and the voice list on the right.

steps.title: Pick a Voice Provider
steps.content:
Click a provider in the left rail to browse its voices. Providers differ in style, latency, and language coverage:

  • iMash TTS — self-hosted, fully tunable, and the home of voice cloning.
  • Cartesia — ultra-low latency, real-time voices with emotion control.
  • ElevenLabs — studio-grade, highly expressive streaming voices.
  • Rime — natural, dialog-style voices.
  • Playht and Deepgram — solid general-purpose text-to-speech.
  • OpenAI — Whisper-tier multilingual voices.
  • Azure — Microsoft neural voices with wide language coverage.
  • Resemble — cloned and custom voices.
  • MiniMax — deeply configurable, multi-language voices.
  • Google — WaveNet cloud voices.
  • Inworld — character and persona voices.
⚠️ If your agent's brain is a Google realtime (speech-to-speech) model, only Google voices are selectable — realtime models speak through their own built-in voice set, so every other provider (and Clone Voice) appears grayed out. Conversely, with any other model the Google entry is disabled.

steps.title: Filter, Preview, and Select a Voice
steps.content:
1. Narrow the list with the Gender, Accent, and Age dropdowns — for example Female + English (US) + Adult.
  2. Click the blue play button on any row to hear a short sample. Click it again to pause.
  3. The table shows each voice's Name, Provider, Gender, Accent (with a language flag), and Age. Use the pagination controls at the bottom to browse — large catalogs run to hundreds of voices.
  4. When you find the one, tick the checkbox in the last column of its row. The card above the table updates to show your new selection — the choice is saved to your agent immediately.
💡 The card to the right of the filters always shows the voice your agent is currently using, with its own play button — a handy sanity check before you hang your brand on it.

images.alt: Filters, current-voice card with play button, and the voice table with play buttons and select checkboxes
images.caption: Filter by Gender, Accent, and Age; preview with the play button; select with the checkbox in the last column.

images.alt: Filters, current-voice card with play button, and the voice table with play buttons and select checkboxes
images.caption: Filter by Gender, Accent, and Age; preview with the play button; select with the checkbox in the last column.

steps.title: Fine-Tune with Advanced Settings
steps.content:
Click Advanced Settings in the provider header to open the tuning popup. The knobs depend on the active provider:

  • Cartesia — Model, Emotion (dozens of moods from happy to sarcastic), Speed (0.5×–2×), and Volume.
  • ElevenLabs — Model, Stability, Clarity and Similarity, Style Exaggeration, Speed (0.8–1.2), Streaming Latency, and Use Speaker Boost.
  • MiniMax — Model, Speed, Volume, Pitch, Emotion, Text Normalization (better digit reading), Intensity, and Timbre.
  • Inworld — Model, Speed, Pitch, and Temperature.
  • Playht, Deepgram, OpenAI, Azure — a Speed slider.
  • iMash TTS — a Custom advanced settings switch. Off (the default) uses the platform's tuned server defaults; on unlocks creative knobs like Output level, Exaggeration (emotion intensity), Temperature, and CFG scale.
  1. Adjust the sliders, then click Done to close the popup — changes apply to your agent right away.
💡 Start with small adjustments. A speed of 1.05 or an exaggeration bump of 0.1 is often all it takes to make a voice feel alive without becoming unstable.

images.alt: Advanced Voice Settings popup showing Model, Emotion, Speed, and Volume controls for Cartesia
images.caption: Advanced Voice Settings for Cartesia: Model, Emotion, Speed, and Volume. Each provider exposes its own knobs.

images.alt: Advanced Voice Settings popup showing Model, Emotion, Speed, and Volume controls for Cartesia
images.caption: Advanced Voice Settings for Cartesia: Model, Emotion, Speed, and Volume. Each provider exposes its own knobs.

steps.title: Set a Backup Voice (Failover)
steps.content:
Even the best cloud provider has a bad day. A backup voice keeps your agent talking if the primary TTS provider errors or times out mid-call.

  1. In the top-right of the voice picker, switch Production | Backup to Backup. A yellow banner confirms: "You're configuring the Backup Voice."
  2. Pick a provider and tick a voice exactly like you did for production — the picker works the same, but everything you choose here applies only to the fallback.
  3. Switch back to Production when you're done.
💡 Choose a backup from a different provider than your production voice — that's the whole point of failover. Pick one that sounds similar so callers barely notice the switch. The configured backup then shows next to the pill on the Voice (TTS) row (e.g. "Backup: cartesia").

images.alt: Voice picker in Backup mode with a warning banner and a backup voice selected
images.caption: Backup mode: choices made here only apply when the production voice fails.

images.alt: Voice picker in Backup mode with a warning banner and a backup voice selected
images.caption: Backup mode: choices made here only apply when the production voice fails.

steps.title: Clone Your Own Voice
steps.content:
Want your agent to sound like you (or your best rep)? Clone a voice from a short audio sample:

  1. Click Clone Voice at the bottom of the left rail.
  2. In the Clone Your Voice card, enter a Voice Name (e.g. "My Voice") and an optional Description.
  3. Choose the Language the sample is spoken in.
  4. Under Audio Sample, either click Record Audio and speak into your microphone (click Stop Recording when done), or click Upload File to use an existing audio clip. Use Clear & Re-record if you want another take.
  5. Click Clone Voice. A progress bar runs while the clone is created, and the new voice appears in the Your Cloned Voices list on the right, where you can play, select, or delete it.
💡 For best results, record 5–10 seconds of clear, natural speech in a quiet room — the recording must be at least 3 seconds long. Your cloned voices also appear under the iMash TTS provider, and when iMash TTS is active a Cloned Voices button in the provider header manages them without leaving the tab.

images.alt: Clone Voice pane with the Clone Your Voice form and the Your Cloned Voices list
images.caption: Clone Voice: name it, record or upload a sample, and click Clone Voice. Your clones are listed on the right.

images.alt: Clone Voice pane with the Clone Your Voice form and the Your Cloned Voices list
images.caption: Clone Voice: name it, record or upload a sample, and click Clone Voice. Your clones are listed on the right.

steps.title: Finish and Hear It Live
steps.content:
1. Click Done in the bottom-right of the voice picker to close it. Voice picks and tuning changes are saved as you make them, so there's nothing else to submit.
  2. Back in the editor, click Test Agent in the top-right (or Connect in the chat pane) and talk to your agent to hear the new voice on a real conversation.

That's it — your agent now answers every call in exactly the voice you chose, with a failover standing by. 🎙️

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
